assessor_1_raw: Dense retrieval experiment: well-designed. Temporal split prevents future terminology leakage. Weak-label audit quantifies noise. Strict label mitigates popularity bias. Hard negative mining from BM25 is standard. Spearman popularity correlation is an excellent diagnostic. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: The retrieval experiment is methodologically sound. Data quality controls are appropriate. Both models receive equal tuning. Coverage and BM25-to-dense overlap are useful secondary metrics. Temporal drift analysis (months 19-21 vs 22-24) is a strong robustness check. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: Strong design. The strict label (click + no rejection) is a principled approach to reduce false positives in relevance labeling. Per-product-area NDCG@5 enables granular confound analysis. Manual audit of 100 test queries where dense outperforms BM25 is an excellent qualitative validation. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous defense_wins from all three assessors.
